channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya's State Repression and Youth Protests in 2026
item.description: The article analyzes Kenya's political landscape in 2026, focusing on the government's heavy-handed response to youth-led protests. It highlights how the state has shifted from reactive crowd control ...

item.title: News Corp Australia blocks access for non-crawler users
item.description: The article details a technical barrier implemented by News Corp Australia to manage crawler bot traffic on their websites. Users encountering a block message are instructed to troubleshoot by disabli...

item.title: Ongoing Disputes in the West Philippine Sea Despite Legal Victory
item.description: A decade after the Philippines secured its historic arbitral victory against China over South China Sea claims, fishermen and activists continue to confront maritime disputes. In Subic Bay, fisherfolk...

item.title: PFIPC Scandal Unfolds as Investigation Reveals Fake Agency's Operations
item.description: Documents show the disowned Director-General of the disputed Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeniyi Adeyemi, manipulated government agencies to secure CBN accounts and op...

item.title: Granit Xhaka's Leadership Journey: From Childhood Sacrifices to World Cup Leadership
item.description: Switzerland captain Granit Xhaka reflects on his formative years, highlighting how early responsibility and his parents' sacrifices shaped his leadership on the World Cup stage. Born in Switzerland to...

item.title: White Supremacist March in Washington, D.C.
item.description: The article discusses a white supremacist march in Washington, D.C., which has sparked debates about the state of democracy in the United States. The event, organized by far-right groups, drew signifi...

item.title: BIR Launches One-Time Tax Abatement Program for Micro Taxpayers
item.description: The Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) has introduced Revenue Regulations No. 4-2026, a one-time tax abatement program aimed at helping eligible micro taxpayers resolve unpaid tax obligations. This init...

item.title: Metro Vancouver Regional Parks Workers Stage Indefinite Strike Over Contract Deadlock
item.description: Over 100 workers with Metro Vancouver's regional parks have walked off the job indefinitely, citing frustration over stalled negotiations for 18 months. The union, Greater Vancouver Regional District ...

item.title: Ronaldo Reflects on Age, Criticism, and Legacy at World Cup
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo, 41, addressed media and fans ahead of Portugal's World Cup clash with Spain, acknowledging his decline in physical ability but emphasizing his mental resilience. The five-time Ballo...

item.title: Israeli PM Netanyahu addresses Christian villages' concerns in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu claimed that some Christian villages in southern Lebanon had requested Israeli annexation to protect them from Hezbollah attacks. The statement came amid ongo...

item.title: NASA Releases Patriotic Space Images for US 250th Anniversary
item.description: To commemorate the United States' 250th anniversary, NASA unveiled four striking images from the Chandra X-ray Observatory, each rendered in patriotic red, white, and blue. These visuals capture diver...

item.title: Review explores how Maria Takolander’s The End of Romance examines violence, motherhood and survival in a dystopian future
item.description: This review examines Maria Takolander’s novel The End of Romance, a dystopian work set on a devastated future Earth where environmental collapse, social decay and endless warfare have reshaped human l...

item.title: Senate Preparing for Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: The Senate majority bloc convened for a caucus on Monday to prepare for the upcoming impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte. The meeting focused on key procedural matters, including the sele...

item.title: England Advances to World Cup Quarterfinals After Dramatic Comeback Against Mexico
item.description: England secured a 3-2 victory over Mexico in a thrilling FIFA World Cup match, overcoming a spirited comeback by the hosts. The game, played at Azteca Stadium in Mexico City, saw England face signific...

item.title: Wildfire near Boston Bar Forces Evacuations and Expands to 1,200 Hectares
item.description: An aggressive wildfire near Boston Bar, British Columbia, has grown from approximately 100 hectares to over 1,200 hectares, prompting evacuation orders and alerts in the Fraser Canyon region. The Brun...

item.title: Robot Malfunction at Workplace Sparks Safety Concerns
item.description: A humanoid robot malfunctioned at a workplace, causing erratic movements and alarming employees during a routine day. The incident, captured in a viral video, shows the robot performing kung fu-like a...

item.title: Fehi Fineanganofo overcomes debut nerves as New Zealand edge France in Test victory
item.description: New Zealand wing Fehi Fineanganofo revealed that the pressure and excitement of making his All Blacks debut left him so nervous that he vomited during the half-time break of his side's 34-32 victory o...

item.title: Southern right whales rest upside-down to manage energy during breeding season
item.description: Southern right whales (Eubalaena australis) are often seen resting upside-down in shallow waters near Australia’s southern coast, a behavior previously misunderstood as an indication of illness. New r...

item.title: England's historic World Cup victory over Mexico: Kane's goal, Henderson's injury, and the chaos of the Azteca clash
item.description: England secured a dramatic 3-2 World Cup victory over Mexico in a match marked by controversy, red cards, and standout performances. Harry Kane, despite losing his voice, celebrated his crucial goal i...

item.title: Travellers urged to take extra precautions as phone and jewellery thefts increase in Bali
item.description: Australian travellers planning a trip to Bali are being encouraged to remain vigilant following reports of an increase in pickpocketing, phone snatching and jewellery theft across several of the islan...
